medical_history.chief_complaint: A life-long history of a hoarse, breathy voice
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Stridor on strenuous exertion, but denied dysphagia, choking or recurrent chest infections. A direct laryngoscopy performed 8 years previously had revealed a small lesion of the right aryepiglottic fold (biopsy showed non-specific inflammatory changes only), but was otherwise reported as normal. Subsequent speech therapy did not improve the voice.
medical_history.past_medical_history: An 8-day period of nasogastric feeding as a premature neonate (indication not recorded in the patient’s notes) and three uneventful endotracheal intubations for unrelated childhood E.N.T. procedures.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Outpatient fibreoptic nasendoscopy revealed scarring of the right supraglottis (aryepiglottic fold) at the site of the previous excision biopsy, but otherwise normal looking, fully mobile vocal cords. A CT scan was performed, which showed a type-2 submucosal PLC, with a deficiency in the interarytenoideus muscle and a defect in the lamina of the cricoid cartilage.
